narrative: Fire regimes feature prominently today: intraspecific variation in serotiny lets a Chinese pine subspecies track fire-prone microhabitats, a tree-ring record ties a 19th-century Appalachian fire pulse to industrialisation rather than climate alone, and a pan-boreal forest-age map links wildfire and logging history to carbon and biodiversity outcomes. On the climate front, a 24,000-plot analysis across eastern North America projects rising background tree mortality under continued warming. Movement and population papers extend step-selection models into three dimensions for swimming and flying animals, link home-range overlap to sociality and territoriality, and show that strong social bonds predict survival in a cooperative bird. At the community and landscape scale, a global assessment quantifies the ecosystem footprint of freshwater fish invasions, ant-mediated seed dispersal is shown to persist in low-intensity tropical croplands, and ancient grasslands are found to hold higher plant genetic diversity than younger ones. In conservation, three decades of IUCN Red List assessments chart an accelerating extinction-risk trajectory for sharks, rays and chimaeras, while a five-year retrospective on structured expert elicitation tests how well predicted management outcomes matched reality. Evolutionary biology contributes a call for climate adaptation practice to learn from natural selection's track record on maladaptation, alongside new biogeographic work on tree-line climatology across Europe and polyploidy's effect on geographic range size in Clarkia.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas
articles.digest: Pine populations in fire-prone parts of southwestern China show greater serotiny and readiness to regenerate after wildfire than those in less fire-exposed sites.

articles.title: Industrialization Drives a Late‐19th‐Century Pulse in Appalachian Fire Activity
articles.digest: Tree-ring evidence shows 19th-century industrialisation, not just climate, drove an extreme spike in Appalachian fire activity and reorganised regional fire patterns.

articles.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
articles.digest: A pan-boreal map of forest age built from 35 years of disturbance data reveals uneven age structure with direct implications for carbon storage and biodiversity conservation.

articles.title: Climate Change Projected to Increase Rates of Background Tree Mortality Across Eastern North America
articles.digest: Across more than 24,000 forest plots in eastern North America, warmer temperatures are linked to higher background tree mortality, a trend projected to worsen with warming.

articles.title: A Drier Year Markedly Enhances Methane, but Not Carbon Dioxide, Emissions in a Mediterranean Reservoir

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: Introduces a new framework combining eDNA inventories, graph theory and niche modelling.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Novel modelling framework linking hunting-pressure drivers to wildlife impacts produces pantropical defaunation maps.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Presents a novel model integrating passive acoustic and point-count data into density surfaces.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: ForestForTrees: new R package inferring forest size-abundance structure from occlusion-corrected remote sensing.

methods_radar.title: sampley: A Python package for sampling visual survey data
methods_radar.note: sampley: new Python package standardising sampling of visual survey data into spatiotemporal units.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Develops a benchmarking workflow comparing species distribution model frameworks for loggerhead turtle conservation.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Combines multi-source data with a machine-learning algorithm to estimate soil organic carbon density.

methods_radar.title: From Deficiency to Enrichment: Diverging Soil Phosphorus Trajectories Across China's Croplands (1980–2018)
methods_radar.note: Integrates harmonised surveys, long-term experiments and machine-learning modelling of phosphorus trajectories.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Multiscale species distribution models applied to reveal seascape niche partitioning in fishes.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Multi-satellite comparison method benchmarks forest growth between karst and non-karst reserves.

methods_radar.title: Discovering the unseen: A performance comparison of taxonomic classification methods for unknown DNA barcodes
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks taxonomic classification methods for unknown DNA barcodes against incomplete reference databases.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: BIMr2 improves on prior BIMr Bayesian method for inferring migration rates from multilocus genotypes.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Systematically addresses false negative and positive detection error reporting in eDNA studies.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Environmental DNA approach advanced to jointly resolve community composition and genetic variation.

methods_radar.title: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs
methods_radar.note: Satellite imagery and machine learning combined to map wetland types and fragmentation across Europe.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to wolf colonisation and persistence in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Uses camera-trap abundance indices to test the abundant-center hypothesis across mammal and bird species.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Applies ground-to-canopy camera trapping to reveal vertically stratified mammal community patterns in Congo Basin.

methods_radar.title: Is There Mush-room for Bettongs on Dirk Hartog Island? Environmental DNA Assessment of Food Resource Availability for Fauna Translocations
methods_radar.note: Applies environmental DNA assessment to evaluate food resources for a fauna translocation.

methods_radar.title: Predicting Coral Offspring Survival From Reef Thermal Histories in Ningaloo World Heritage Reef
methods_radar.note: Applies boosted regression tree analysis to satellite temperature data to predict coral larval survival.

methods_radar.title: eDNA sampling reveals no evidence of negative effects of beaver recolonisation on the catchment‐scale distribution of migratory fish
methods_radar.note: Applies catchment-scale eDNA sampling to assess beaver and migratory fish co-distribution.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: Applies UAV-based remote sensing to study urban vegetation cooling of private gardens.

methods_radar.title: Quaternary Ice Ages Shaped Protists Phylogeography: The Case of Arcellinida in the Iberian Peninsula
methods_radar.note: Applies metabarcoding dataset (ArKOI) to test the genetic-legacy-of-Quaternary paradigm in testate amoebae.

methods_radar.title: Tropical forest restoration can offset water flux losses from deforestation but not everywhere
methods_radar.note: Uses satellite-derived evapotranspiration and precipitation observations to test forest restoration's offset of water flux losses.

methods_radar.title: Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
methods_radar.note: Applies joint species distribution modelling to test trait-environment transferability between native and exotic plants.
